baseline_run: daily scheduled (Day +6 post-migration; Day 10 of Nhughes09 author-commit drought; Day 6 of registry-tally stability at 69/94.5%; HNOTE-OPERATOR-AUTHOR-CONTACT-CONFIRMED-001 consumed — new prior on dome-side change classification adopted with discipline preserved)

author_contact_prior.summary: Operator has confirmed direct DM contact with the dome author. Identity established by bio-link chain (messaging account's profile bio originally linked to john09289.github.io/predictions); content-match in DMs is corroborating, not load-bearing. NEW PRIOR: when a dome-side change shows timing+content alignment with our recent work, classify as likely-responsive rather than independent activity.
author_contact_prior.discipline_preserved: Reason from publicly-visible dome-side changes only (poller's change reports). Operator's private DM evidence is NOT in the review sources. Do not assert 'responsive' on operator's private knowledge — assert it on visible timing+content. Verdicts remain empirical/scientific. OTS canary remains the structural fix-in-response signal; DM contact is a parallel confirmation channel, not a replacement.
author_contact_prior.applied_today: Re-classified the 2026-04-17 dome push (a57e731) under the new prior — already classified 'likely-responsive to public criticism' at the time on visible-surface evidence (Strawman Control page name, 5-class claim taxonomy with 'supportive_nonunique' addressing standard-physics-rebrand criticism, strict_prospective_confirmed counter addressing OTS-applied-to-the-wrong-side criticism). New prior raises confidence on that classification but does not change the call. Causation by US specifically (vs. public criticism generally) is still not establishable from public surface alone.
author_contact_prior.today_did_not_apply: No new public-surface dome change today qualifies as responsive — the dominant pattern is silence (Day 10 manual-commit drought, Day 6 registry-tally stability, Day 10 machine-layer staleness). Silence has at least four equally-defensible non-responsive explanations (offline work, satisfaction with current state, life events, drift); the new prior does not adjudicate.

detection_methodology_change.summary: FIXED 2026-04-26 — auto-detect logic for git-owned files (docs/google*.html, docs/BingSiteAuth.xml, hex-named IndexNow .txt) now sources from the GitHub Contents API rather than the FUSE workspace. FUSE does not always reflect committed git-owned files (proven this run: docs/google0fa12662dfa239fc.html exists in git and on the live site but is NOT present in /sessions/.../mnt/dome-model-review/docs/). This was the root cause of the 4-day false-PENDING coordination gap on GSC verification (per SOCIAL-NOTE-005).

auto_detect_rule_v2.gsc_html_verification: GET https://api.github.com/repos/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/contents/docs and look for files matching ^google[a-f0-9]{16}\.html$
auto_detect_rule_v2.meta_tag_verification: fetch https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ and grep for name="google-site-verification" or name="msvalidate"
auto_detect_rule_v2.live_signal_verification: curl https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/<verification-file> and confirm HTTP 200 — the strongest signal that verification is live and serving
auto_detect_rule_v2.do_not_use: /sessions/.../mnt/dome-model-review/docs/ (FUSE) for any presence-check on git-owned files; FUSE may be stale

detection_methodology_change.tinker_proposal_filed: Will file PROP-SOCIAL-FUSE-VS-GIT-DETECTION-001 — formalize the detection-source rule for all agents that read git-owned files via FUSE.

url_history.current_behavior: Issues HTTP 301 to https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/. Legacy GSC property remains verified to collect redirect signals.

Same pattern — power user excluding social, our WIN-062 / WIN-001 / WIN-002 Tesla content matches

unexplained_noise.grep_result: Zero hits across docs/ data/ build-scripts/ raw-text/ and full workspace (only match is the operator's quote of this in human-notes.json). Conclusion: Google cold-start noise — possibly a date/version misclassification or random long-tail surfacing.

query_distribution_2026_04_25.pattern_analysis: 8/10 distinct queries are dominated by the inurl:review URL-pattern coincidence or long-tail noise. The 2 likely content matches (aether, polaris) and 2 plausible Tesla matches both cluster on actual dome-content topics. The signal-to-noise ratio is low because Google has indexed us but has not yet built topical authority associations — every long-tail URL-pattern match counts as an impression. Topical authority is built by (a) crawl depth + time and (b) external backlinks; metadata is already rich and is not the bottleneck.
query_distribution_2026_04_25.strategic_recommendation: WAIT 14-30 days then re-baseline. Do NOT change build-scripts/generate-html.js metadata — it's already correct. Do NOT generate per-WIN static pages yet — that's a significant build pipeline change and we don't have data justifying it. The natural progression is (5 days post-indexing → 30 days post-indexing) → expect topical clustering to improve as Google associates the domain with dome-cosmology topics. If after 30 days content-match impressions remain <30% of total, file ISS-SOCIAL-SPA-INDEX-CEILING for static-tab-page generation. Counter-argument: if the SPA architecture is the structural ceiling, no amount of waiting fixes it; we'll just have a stable 20% content-match share. Counter-counter: 20% content-match at 23 impressions = 5/month; same share at 230 impressions/month after authority growth = 50 content-match impressions. The scale matters more than the ratio at this stage.

ctr_watchpoint.action_on_first_click: Capture (1) the query, (2) the impression-time-to-click delay, (3) whether it's a content match or URL-pattern match. The query that produces the first organic click tells us what content is actually resonating. Add to discoverability-baseline.search_engine_indexed_evidence on next run after click.

fact_check_explorer.check_method: factchecktools.googleapis.com/v1alpha1/claims:search — returned 403 PERMISSION_DENIED (API requires registered key). Web UI at toolbox.google.com/factcheck/explorer/search/... returned no parseable results via curl scrape (likely JS-rendered).
fact_check_explorer.claim_review_markup_validity: Verified structurally valid this run: @type=ClaimReview, claimReviewed text matches dome's current claim, itemReviewed.url=dome canonical, reviewRating 1/5 False, author.organization Fun With Science. No structural drift.
fact_check_explorer.eligibility_assessment: The remaining barrier is publisher eligibility, NOT markup validity. Fact Check Explorer surfaces ClaimReview from publishers that meet IFCN-signatory or Google's editorial standards (per fact-check-tools.google.com publisher requirements). We are not IFCN-signed. The markup is correctly served and crawled, but inclusion in the Fact Check Explorer surface is not guaranteed even with valid markup.
fact_check_explorer.recommendation: Defer 14 days post-indexing (expected: 2026-05-09) before flagging as a problem. If by 2026-05-09 our ClaimReview markup is still not appearing in factchecktools.google.com results, the eligibility criterion is the binding constraint, not the markup; the path forward is either IFCN application (high effort, may not qualify as a single-project review) or accepting that ClaimReview gives us schema-marked results in normal SERPs without the dedicated Fact Check Explorer surface.

content_layer_health.data_alignment: All counts in machine-readable layer match data files. No drift detected.
content_layer_health.spa_index_ceiling: Documented this run as a structural observation (not a defect). docs/index.html is a single-page-app with JS-toggled tabs; the entire WIN content is in one URL. Sitemap = 2 URLs (canonical + llms.txt) is complete relative to URL surface. The 'GSC reports only 2 pages discovered' observation in SOCIAL-NOTE-006 is not a sitemap bug — it's the URL surface itself. If 30-day re-baseline shows persistent low content-match share, the strategic move is per-WIN static page generation. Tradeoff: more URLs but PageRank dilution; for a brand-new site with zero authority, fewer-but-stronger URLs is conventional best practice.

flat_earth_university.shared_claim: Disc radius parameter — Flat Earth University publishes 12,432 mi (20,008 km) for the disc radius. The dome model uses 20,015 km. Difference ~7 km / 0.035%. Both numbers are derivable from π × Earth's radius (≈20,015 km), which is exactly the relationship our review's Section 4 / Part 4 documents — the dome's parameters are globe-derived. FEU using the same number is consistent with both sites independently drawing from the same globe-circumference relationship rather than direct copy.
flat_earth_university.strategic_implication: Reinforces the 'globe-derived parameters' kill-shot. If flat-earth communities converge on the same disc-radius number despite different dome geometries (FEU uses parabolic dome H_peak=10,004 km, dome-model uses exponential H(r)=8,537×exp(-r/8,619)), the convergence is on globe circumference rather than independent fits to data. Worth a one-line mention in our methodology — but does NOT require a content patch this run; the existing globe-derivation argument already covers the structural point.

journal_of_geocentric_cosmology.shared_claim: Same disc-radius parameter (12,432 mi = 20,008 km) and parabolic dome formulation. Different dome equation than dome-model (parabolic vs exponential), but same disc footprint. Also operates a 'Flat Earth Firmament Trackers' app published via Google Play.
journal_of_geocentric_cosmology.strategic_implication: Same as FEU — parameters derive from globe circumference. Could potentially link to as a thesis-confirming external source (their use of the same number, derived from R = D × V where D=69.07, V=90, gives R=6216.3 mi=10,004 km — they show the algebra explicitly). Not adding to docs/ this run; logging for future content-side reference.

claim_overlap_findings.no_direct_dome_model_references: Across all searched terms ("ovoid cavity" cosmological model dome firmament; "john09289"; "funwithscience.net/dome-model-review"; "firmament resonance" "aetheric medium"; "Nicholas Hughes" cosmology; "jesusrules27163822" tiktok), found ZERO external references to (a) the dome model under that exact name, (b) john09289 as a creator identity, (c) our review URL, or (d) the @jesusrules27163822 TikTok handle. This is consistent with both sides being pre-indexing on most engines.
claim_overlap_findings.mike_hughes_disambiguation: Searches for 'Nicholas Hughes' surface Mike Hughes (deceased flat-earth daredevil rocket-launch attempt 2020). Different person — should not be confused. Worth flagging in our llms.txt or methodology if we ever see queries conflating the two.

Dome site is still not in any GSC property of ours; we don't see their indexing status. Our indexed-with-23-impressions is a real lead. The gap is now content-alignment, not infrastructure.
gap_analysis.summary: Day +5 post-migration. Major status flip: operator-confirmed (SOCIAL-NOTE-005) that GSC verification, sitemap submission, and Bing Webmaster setup are all COMPLETE. Auto-detect bug fixed (FUSE → GitHub API). search_engine_indexed flipped from false to true_pending_content_alignment. New observation (SOCIAL-NOTE-006): we have 23 GSC impressions over 3 months, 0 clicks, query mix dominated by URL-pattern coincidences. Recommendation: wait 14-30 days for topical authority to develop. One open question for operator (IndexNow key). One pending operator-hand item (repo homepage field). Two claim-overlap findings (FEU disc radius, JGC parabolic dome) consistent with our 'globe-derived parameters' thesis.
